target.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping_incident" and r._field == "rtt_max")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "target_name", "type"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with text: r.target_name + " " + r.type + " (max=" + string(v: r._value) + "ms)", tags: r.type }))

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "netmon_event" and r._field == "description")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "category", "source"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with text: r._value, tags: r.category + "," + r.source }))

from(bucket: "netmon_speedtest")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "iperf3")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "jitter_ms" or r._field == "loss_pct" or r._field == "recv_mbps" or r._field == "duration_sec")
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "server"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      time: r._time,
      timeEnd: time(v: int(v: r._time) + int(v: r.duration_sec) * 1000000000),
      text: "iperf3 → " + r.server + ": recv=" + string(v: r.recv_mbps) + " Mbps, jitter=" + string(v: r.jitter_ms) + " ms, loss=" + string(v: r.loss_pct) + "%",
      tags: "iperf3"
  }))

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -24h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping_incident" and r._field == "rtt_max")
  |> count()
  |> group()
  |> sum()

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -24h)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping_incident" and r._field == "rtt_max")
  |> group(columns: ["type"])
  |> count()

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -5m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eap_mesh" and r.iface == "apclix0" and r._field == "snr")
  |> last()

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -5m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "wifi_station" and r._field == "signal_dbm")
  |> last()

panels.title: Latency — All Targets (red dashes = incidents)
panels.description: Per-target ping latency from the Pi. Annotations (red) mark moments when an incident was triggered. Gateway/EAP staying flat while internet targets all spike together = WAN issue. Only one internet target spiking = transient (target-side or sticky session). Multiple but not all internet targets spiking = likely one WAN degraded (load balancer hash).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping" and r._field == "rtt_max")
  |> group(columns: ["target_name"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: max, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Packet Loss — All Targets
panels.description: Concurrent loss across multiple internet targets (1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8, 9.9.9.9, 4.2.2.2) is the cleanest signal of an upstream issue. Loss only on gateway/eap = local/mesh problem.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping" and r._field == "loss_pct")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._value > 0.0)
  |> group(columns: ["target_name"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: max, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Pi-side vs Router-side Ping (during incidents)
panels.description: Each red dot is router_ping (from router during an incident). If router latency is normal while Pi-side spiked = problem is Pi-to-router (WiFi/mesh). If both spike = upstream of router.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping" and r._field == "rtt_avg" and (r.target_name == "dns_google" or r.target_name == "dns_cloudflare"))
  |> group(columns: ["target_name"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _field: "Pi → " + r.target_name }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_ping" and r._field == "rtt_avg")
  |> group(columns: ["target"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _field: "Router → " + r.target }))

panels.title: Mesh Backhaul SNR (signal-as-proxy, see note)
panels.description: NOTE: SNR absolute value is unreliable on EAP610 (MediaTek driver reports hardcoded noise). Use it to spot RELATIVE drops during incident windows — a sudden dip from 'baseline' to lower is signal. Persistent low values do not mean mesh is broken; cross-check with mesh bitrate and EAP latency on the connectivity dashboard.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "eap_mesh" and r._field == "snr")
  |> group(columns: ["name"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Pi WiFi Signal & TX Bitrate
panels.description: If the Pi's own WiFi signal drops or TX bitrate collapses during an incident, the issue is local to the Pi's WiFi adapter — not representative of other clients.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "wifi_station" and r._field == "signal_dbm")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _field: "signal_dbm" }))

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "wifi_station" and r._field == "tx_bitrate")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _field: "tx_bitrate" }))

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping_incident" and (r._field == "rtt_max" or r._field == "loss_pct" or r._field == "jitter"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "target_name", "type", "target"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "target_name", "type", "rtt_max", "loss_pct", "jitter"])
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"], desc: true)
  |> limit(n: 100)
